# Feature: Flows Pane Actions

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-004  
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_pane_actions.md`

Add an explicit confirmation step before deleting a flow from the Flows pane while preserving existing row behavior (run hover action, rename flow inline, and adapter-backed persistence).

This feature reduces accidental destructive actions and brings Flows delete behavior into parity with the pane action standards established in the parent plan.

---

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

`.spec-lite/data_model.md` does not exist. This feature uses existing flow and UI-state models and introduces no new persisted entities.

### Entities & Attributes

- **`Flow`** (existing persisted model)
  - `id` (string) - stable flow identifier used for selection, rename, run, and delete.
  - `name` (string) - flow display name used in list rows and confirmation text.
  - `nodes` (`FlowNode[]`) - used to determine whether run action is available (`nodes.length > 0`).
  - `connectors` (`FlowConnector[]`) - part of persisted flow graph, unchanged by this feature.
  - `updatedAt` (ISO string) - row metadata shown in Flows pane.

- **`FlowsSlice`** (existing Zustand slice state)
  - `flows` (`Flow[]`) - source list rendered by `FlowsPane`.
  - `flowRunStates` (`Record<string, FlowRunState>`) - controls `Running...` row state and action visibility.
  - `removeFlow(id)` - removes a flow and related run/dirty state after successful adapter deletion.
  - `isFlowNameUnique(name, excludeId?)` - existing rename uniqueness constraint.

- **`FlowsPane` local UI state** (existing + reused)
  - `searchText` (string) - client-side filter term.
  - `editingFlowId` (`string | null`) - currently inline-edited flow row.
  - `editingName` (string) - in-progress rename text.

- **`useConfirmDialog` state** (existing shared hook)
  - `isOpen` (boolean) - whether confirm modal is shown.
  - `isConfirming` (boolean) - async confirmation progress.
  - `options` (`ConfirmDialogOptions | null`) - title/message/confirm callback for current delete target.

### Relationships

- `FlowsSlice.flows` 1:N rendered rows in `FlowsPane`.
- `flowRunStates` keyed by `Flow.id` drives row action behavior (`Running...` vs run/menu controls).
- One confirm dialog action maps to one `Flow` delete target (`id` + `name`) at a time.

### Indexes & Constraints

- Flow names remain unique case-insensitively via existing `isFlowNameUnique`; FEAT-004 does not alter rename rules.
- Delete must be adapter-first: call `storageAdapter.deleteFlow(flowId)` only after explicit confirmation.
- Store mutation (`removeFlow`) must happen only when adapter delete succeeds (`result.isOk === true`).

---

## 3. Files

List of files this feature is expected to create or modify:

- `packages/core/src/components/common/FlowsPane.tsx` - integrate `useConfirmDialog` into delete action flow and render `ConfirmDialogComponent`.
- `packages/core/src/test/components/common/FlowsPane.test.tsx` - component tests for FEAT-004 delete-confirmation behavior and row action regression coverage.

---

## 4. Dependencies

- **FEAT-001**: Shared confirmation hook `useConfirmDialog` (already implemented).
- Existing storage adapter contract: `deleteFlow(flowId)` returning `Result<void, string>`.
- Existing flows store actions/selectors: `removeFlow`, `isFlowRunning`, `isFlowNameUnique`, `setCurrentEditingFlowId`.

---

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Add confirm-before-delete flow for Flows pane deletion

- [ ] **Implementation**: Update `FlowsPane.tsx` to use `useConfirmDialog`. Replace direct Delete menu invocation (`handleDeleteFlow`) with `openConfirmDialog(...)` carrying flow-specific title/message and an async `onConfirm` callback that calls `storageAdapter.deleteFlow`. Keep adapter-success gating: call `removeFlow` and success notification only after successful delete; show error notification and reject on failure so the dialog remains open.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Add tests that verify (1) clicking Delete opens confirmation dialog, (2) cancel does not call adapter delete, (3) confirm calls `deleteFlow` and removes flow from store-rendered list, (4) adapter failure shows error notification and leaves flow intact.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Add/update inline comments and JSDoc in `FlowsPane.tsx` explaining why deletion is confirm-gated and why store mutation occurs only after adapter success.
- **Verify**: Run `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- FlowsPane` and manually confirm deleting a flow requires explicit confirmation.

### TASK-002: Preserve existing Flows row behaviors while introducing confirmation

- [ ] **Implementation**: Ensure FEAT-004 does not regress existing row interactions in `FlowsPane.tsx`: keep hover Run button behavior for runnable flows, keep inline rename workflow and uniqueness validation, keep `Running...` display for active flows, and keep row click selection/editing behavior.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Add/extend tests verifying (1) run action remains available for flows with nodes when not running, (2) rename action still opens inline editor, (3) running flows still show `Running...` and hide run/menu controls, (4) delete path now routes through confirm dialog without affecting other actions.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Update component comments in `FlowsPane.tsx` to reflect preserved hover-run behavior and newly guarded delete flow.
- **Verify**: Render pane with mixed flow states (runnable, empty, running) and confirm no action regressions after introducing delete confirmation.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001.

### TASK-003: Add focused FEAT-004 regression test suite for Flows pane actions

- [ ] **Implementation**: Create `packages/core/src/test/components/common/FlowsPane.test.tsx` using `AdapterProvider`, `createMockAdapter`, and deterministic Zustand state seeding. Mock UI primitives (Dropdown/Tooltip/Dialog) consistently with existing pane tests for stable JSDOM interaction.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Cover FEAT-004 acceptance criteria end-to-end: confirm dialog lifecycle, non-destructive cancel behavior, success and failure delete branches, and preservation of existing flow row actions (run/rename/running state).
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Add a file-level test docblock documenting FEAT-004 regression intent and protected scenarios.
- **Verify**: Run `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- FlowsPane` and ensure all FEAT-004 tests pass.
- **Depends on**: TASK-002.

---

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A.
- **Error Handling**: Use the existing Result-pattern branch handling for adapter calls. On delete failure, surface user-facing errors via `notification.showNotification('error', ...)` and do not remove local/store state.
- **Logging**: No new logging strategy required. Continue existing adapter/service error logging conventions for failed storage operations.

---

- [x] TASK-002: Preserve existing Flows row behaviors while introducing confirmation
